flow_points: India dominated the Powerplay, scoring 92 runs without loss at a rapid 15.33 run rate.
SV Samson anchored India's innings, contributing a significant 34.9% of the team's total runs.
JDS Neesham was New Zealand's key wicket-taker with 3 wickets for 46 runs.
New Zealand's chase started poorly, losing 3 wickets in the Powerplay for only 52 runs and failing to build a stable foundation.
CV Varun had an expensive outing for India, conceding 14.33 runs per over, significantly above his career average (+6.97).
Abhishek Sharma delivered a match-defining spell for India, achieving an excellent economy of 5.0 compared to his career average of 8.87.
AR Patel provided crucial breakthroughs for India, taking 3 wickets for 27 runs.
JJ Bumrah delivered a pivotal spell for India, securing 4 wickets for just 15 runs across 4 overs.
Bumrah's spell was particularly impactful due to an outstanding economy of 3.75, greatly suppressing New Zealand's scoring.
New Zealand's innings culminated in a late-innings collapse, struggling under extreme chase pressure (Index: 90.0).
India secured a comprehensive victory by 96 runs.
